title: Date-Night Chewy Saudi Arabian Tofu Tagine
description: A rich, umami-filled dish inspired by the flavors of Saudi Arabia, featuring tender tofu and a sweet and spicy sauce. Serves as a pescatarian-friendly alternative to traditional meat-based tagines.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the tofu and cook, stirring occasionally, until golden brown on all sides, about 6 minutes.
Add the cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and red pepper flakes, and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the date syrup, pomegranate molasses, and lemon juice, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together, about 10 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Stir in the chopped parsley and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Transfer the tagine to a serving dish and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Carve the tagine into thick slices and serve with couscous or rice.
Garnish with toasted almonds or pistachios, if desired.
Let the tagine rest for 5 minutes before carving and serving.
